Co-authored-by: devstress <30769729+devstress@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Kafka CI failures: bypass Aspire TCP proxy with isProxied: false

Root cause: Aspire's TCP proxy (randomly assigned port 35461) doesn't
properly relay Kafka's binary protocol. ApiVersionRequest consistently
times out through the proxy. Additionally, KAFKA_CFG_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS
hardcodes localhost:29094, which doesn't match the proxy's dynamic port.

Fix: Set isProxied: false on the Kafka endpoint so Docker maps port
29094 directly to container port 9094, bypassing the proxy entirely.
This ensures the advertised listener address matches the actual host port.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devstress/My3DLearning/sessions/698b16d3-177d-4c3f-b6b3-e24a19e34316

Co-authored-by: devstress <30769729+devstress@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Kafka CI: switch from nonexistent bitnami/kafka:3.9.0 to apache/kafka:3.9.0

Root causes:
1. bitnami/kafka:3.9.0 image no longer exists on Docker Hub, so the
   container was never created by Aspire DCP
2. Apache Kafka image requires ://:PORT format (not ://0.0.0.0:PORT)
   in listeners, otherwise it rejects the config at startup
3. isProxied: false with direct port mapping (host:29092 → container:9092)
   is needed because Aspire's ReferenceExpression doesn't resolve the
   proxy port in container env vars

commit.message:
Implement Function.prototype.toString source text preservation

Add byte offset tracking to tokenizer, source span fields to AST function
expression variants, and thread original source text through the compiler
to populate fn_source_texts. This enables Function.prototype.toString to
return the original source text for function expressions and object literal
methods, matching the ECMAScript spec requirement.

- TokenData gains byte_offset field with precomputed byte offset array
- Expr::Function/GeneratorFunction/AsyncFunction/AsyncGeneratorFunction
  gain Option<(usize, usize)> source span field
- Parser computes (start_byte, end_byte) spans for all function/method sites
- Compiler extracts source text via spans and records in fn_source_texts
- VM toString handler already checks fn_source_texts first (no changes needed)

Fixes built-ins/Function/prototype/toString cluster (80/80 pass).
Updates 2 Rust integration tests for new source text behavior.

* Initial plan

* feat: build complete OasisAI production-ready monorepo

- shared/schemas: Pydantic v2 models (ChunkMetadata, CodeChunk, Graph models, LLM models, request/response schemas)
- storage/chroma: ChromaDB HTTP client wrapper (upsert, query, delete_by_repo)
- services/oasis-llm: Ollama wrapper (generate + embed) + FastAPI service
- services/oasis-indexer: RepoScanner + CodeChunker + IndexPipeline + FastAPI service
- services/oasis-graph: PythonASTParser + GraphBuilder + FastAPI service

commit.message:
Add PeerLink design: persistent multiplexed WebSocket transport

One WebSocket per neighbor carries all traffic: API calls, terminal
PTY streams, health checks, MCP tool calls. Binary wire protocol
with 6-byte header, channel multiplexing for concurrent streams.

Key design points:
- Channels 0 (heartbeat), 1-99 (API), 100+ (PTY streams)
- PeerLinkTransport is a drop-in Transport replacement
- Auto-upgrade: nodes negotiate PeerLink if both support it
- Connection count = MST edges (N-1), regardless of session count
- Backward compatible: falls back to MCPTransport for old nodes

4-phase implementation: core multiplexer → transport adapter →
WebSocket endpoint → auto-upgrade negotiation.

Fix SYSLIB1045 code fixer generating duplicate MyRegex names across partial class declarations (#124698)

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/77409

## Problem

When the SYSLIB1045 `BatchFixer` applies multiple code fixes
concurrently to separate partial class declarations, each fix
independently checks existing members via the semantic model. Since all
fixers see the **original** compilation (before any fix is applied),
they all conclude `MyRegex` is available and generate duplicate property
names, causing CS0756.

## Fix

In `UpgradeToGeneratedRegexCodeFixer.CreateGeneratedRegexProperty`,
after finding the first available name via the semantic model, the new
`CountPrecedingRegexCallSites` helper scans all
`DeclaringSyntaxReferences` of the containing type for Regex
constructor/static-method call sites that would also generate new
property names. These are sorted deterministically (by file path, then
span position). The current node's index in that sorted list determines
how many names to skip, giving each concurrent fixer a unique name.

## Test

Added `CodeFixerGeneratesUniqueNamesAcrossPartialClassDeclarations` —
two `new Regex(...)` calls in separate partial class declarations of the
same type, verifying `MyRegex` and `MyRegex1` are generated. Verified
the test fails without the fix (both declarations produce `MyRegex`).

* chore: PRD: Phase 2 — Wire generate-playbook into the build (closes #118)

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #124

- _discover_overlay_variables: handle "var | default(false)" pattern
  so laptop overlay variable is correctly discovered

  Jinja2 ({"bluetooth": bluetooth} not {bluetooth: bluetooth})
- write_playbook: deep copy _SECTION_DEFINITIONS to prevent mutation
  across multiple calls
- write_playbook: use profile-defined tags from role_cond.tags instead
  of deriving from role name (preserves [fonts] tag on nerd-fonts)

  argparse registration
- Update generate-playbook tests for file-write mode
- Regenerate play.yml with fixed generator

* fix: correct Jinja2 combine() pattern in host_vars_json template

Move the 'if cond else {}' inside combine() to prevent data loss
when later overlay variables are not defined. The pattern:
  | combine({...}) if cond else {}
could discard all previously accumulated vars when 'cond' is False.
The correct pattern is:
  | combine({...} if cond else {})

Fix applied in both _generate_host_vars_json_template() and play.yml.

commit.message:
Restrict named capture group names to ASCII letters/digits (#130)

Match java.util.regex.Pattern rules: first character must be an ASCII
letter (A-Z, a-z), subsequent characters must be ASCII letters or
digits (A-Z, a-z, 0-9). Previously SafeRE was too lenient, accepting
underscores, Unicode letters/digits, and connector punctuation, and
also allowing names to start with digits.

Fixes #129

Co-authored-by: suasuasuasuasua <72476123+suasuasuasuasua@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(nvim): only load view once per buffer using view_activated flag

The previous implementation called loadview on every BufWinEnter,
causing repeated disk reads each time you switched between windows.
Now uses a per-buffer view_activated flag (AstroNvim-style) so:
- loadview runs only once per buffer, on first entry
- filetype check ensures treesitter has set up folding before restoring
- mkview is only called when the view has been activated, avoiding
  overwriting views for buffers that were never loaded
- BufWritePost and WinLeave added as additional save triggers

- bootstrap.sh: new entry point (online/offline/no-mise modes)
- home/dot_zshrc.tmpl: Zsh config with kill-switch-aware aliases

- home/dot_ssh/config.tmpl: SSH config with WSL/enterprise conditionals
- home/dot_config/{starship,atuin,bottom,ripgrep}: tool configs

- scripts/install-apt-deps.sh: Tier 1/3/4 apt dependency installer
